frameworks.name: Random Forest classifier
frameworks.one_line: Tree-ensemble classifier trained on resting-state fMRI to predict seizure outcome.

frameworks.name: Support Vector Machine
frameworks.one_line: Alternative classifier used as validation baseline.

frameworks.name: Neural Network classifier
frameworks.one_line: Second alternative classifier used as validation baseline.

frameworks.name: resting-state functional connectivity biomarker
frameworks.one_line: Framework treating rs-fMRI connectivity as biomarker of epileptogenesis.

core_question: Can resting-state fMRI connectivity features predict seizure susceptibility after traumatic brain injury?
key_assumptions: rs-fMRI connectivity carries signal about epileptogenesis risk
post-traumatic epilepsy is a discrete binary outcome learnable from imaging
manipulations: choice of classifier (Random Forest vs SVM vs Neural Network)
train/test split (70/30) across 100 stratified cross-validation rounds
key_findings: Random Forest achieved 69% test accuracy on seizure outcome
SVM and Neural Network classifiers gave comparable validation results
interpretations: rs-fMRI connectivity may serve as a biomarker for identifying high-risk TBI patients
